root_text.an10.154:1.1: “Sacchikātabbañca vo, bhikkhave, dhammaṁ desessāmi na sacchikātabbañca.
root_text.an10.154:1.3: katamo ca, bhikkhave, na sacchikātabbo dhammo?
root_text.an10.154:1.5: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, na sacchikātabbo dhammo.

translation_text.an10.154:1.1: “I will teach you the principle to realize and the principle not to realize. …
translation_text.an10.154:1.3: And what is the principle not to realize?
translation_text.an10.154:1.4: Wrong view, wrong thought, wrong speech, wrong action, wrong livelihood, wrong effort, wrong mindfulness, wrong immersion, wrong knowledge, and wrong freedom.
translation_text.an10.154:1.5: This is called the principle not to realize.
translation_text.an10.154:1.6: And what is the principle to realize?
translation_text.an10.154:1.7: Right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, right immersion, right knowledge, and right freedom.
translation_text.an10.154:1.8: This is called the principle to realize.”
